question_text: Which statement best evaluates why the UK is moving away from coal and nuclear towards renewable energy sources like wind and solar, despite these renewables being less reliable?

options.label: A
options.text: Wind and solar are cheaper per unit of electricity produced than coal and nuclear

options.label: B
options.text: Renewable sources don't produce radioactive waste or CO₂ emissions, reducing long-term environmental and health costs, even though battery storage and backup systems are needed

options.label: C
options.text: Wind turbines and solar panels never need replacing, making them a one-time investment

options.label: D
options.text: Renewable energy produces more power per hour than fossil fuels and nuclear combined

explanation: Option B correctly identifies that despite reliability challenges, renewables avoid long-term environmental damage (no greenhouse gases, no radioactive waste) and health costs from pollution, making them worth the investment in supporting technology like batteries. Options A, C, and D are false—renewables aren't always cheaper upfront, do need replacement, and don't produce more power overall.
